items.title: Roundtables: Can AI Learn to Understand the World?

items.title: Spotify and Universal Music strike deal allowing fan-made AI covers and remixes
items.summary: Spotify is partnering with Universal Music Group￼ to let Premium subscribers create AI-generated song covers and remixes, with participating artists receiving a share of the revenue.

items.title: Six search engines worth trying now that Google isn’t really Google anymore
items.summary: Google is about to look really different, and if you're not a fan of the AI overview feature, then you're not going to like what's coming.

items.title: Scaling creativity in the age of AI
items.summary: Storytelling is core to humanity’s DNA, stemming from our impulse to express ideals, warnings, hopes, and experiences. Technology has always been woven through the medium and the distribution: from early humans&#8…

items.title: Trump delays AI security executive order, saying language ‘could have been a blocker’
items.summary: President Trump delayed signing an executive order that would have required pre-release government security reviews of AI models, citing dissatisfaction with the order's language.

items.title: Spotify adds AI-powered Q&A and briefing generation features to podcasts
items.summary: Spotify will let you generate daily or weekly briefs based on your prompts

items.title: Spotify takes on Google’s NotebookLM with its new app
items.summary: Spotify is releasing the new desktop app as a research preview in more than 20 markets.

items.title: Spotify launches an ElevenLabs-powered audiobook creation tool
items.summary: The AI-powered audiobook generation won't bind authors to an exclusive contract, meaning they are free to publish their generated audiobooks anywhere.

items.title: Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not
items.summary: The vibes were strong at Code with Claude, Anthropic’s two-day event for software developers in London that kicked off on May 19, the same day as Google’s I/O in Palo Alto. (A coincidence, not a flex, Anthropic staffers…

items.title: The Path, founded by Tony Robbins and Calm alums, hopes to offer safer AI therapy
items.summary: The Path says its AI model has scored 95 on the mental health safety AI benchmark, Vera-MH. This compares to a top score of 65 for the consumer bots.

items.title: Hark raises $700M Series A for its secretive ‘universal’ AI interface
items.summary: Hark expects to release its first multimodal models this summer, which it says will power a personal AI platform that works with existing products and services. The company expects to follow that with hardware devices b…

items.title: Google is pitching an AI agent ecosystem to consumers who may not buy it
items.summary: One of the most promising introductions at Google’s I/O developer conference on Tuesday was a new way for consumers to use the web: AI agents. Unfortunately, it was also the most confusing.

items.title: With aluminum prices up 20%, recycling startups bet on AI to cash in
items.summary: Recycling startups are using AI to improve the recovery of critical minerals like aluminum, aiming to build a massive source of the metal.

items.title: AdventHealth advances whole-person care with OpenAI
items.summary: AdventHealth is using ChatGPT for Healthcare to streamline workflows, reduce administrative burden, and return more time to patient care.

items.title: Jensen Huang says he’s found a ‘brand new’ $200B market for Nvidia
items.summary: The next big thing for Nvidia will be CPUs for AI agents, $200 billion worth, CEO Jensen Huang predicts.

items.title: Anthropic says it’s about to have its first profitable quarter
items.summary: Anthropic has told its investors that it will more than double revenue to around $10.9 billion in its second quarter.
